description: Batch 005 operational metadata for CMP116 Eq. (2.29) and the Cammarota CMP85 extraction route. These are navigation cards, not primary-source evidence.

repo_operational_crosswalk.title: LLM-facing source-to-Lean dependency crosswalk for THE-ERIKSSON-PROGRAMME
repo_operational_crosswalk.author: Generated source-db batch 002
repo_operational_crosswalk.license_note: Repository metadata only; not a mathematical source. Use to navigate primary source entries, not as proof.

citations.summary: Batch 005 live-field matrix for CMP116 Eq. (2.29): exact Cammarota theorem, D-family dictionary, threshold dependencies, and product metric comparison remain the live fields.
extracted_claims: CMP116 Eq. (2.29) is visually located and records the D-family product bound, but the external Cammarota theorem and exact D-family dictionary remain source-pending.
The Cammarota Springer metadata confirms the paper and abstract-level relevance, not the theorem constants or hypotheses.

Archive-private holdings and the source-citation catalog now record one visual Cammarota Theorem 1 premise field, Eq. (1.4), but the theorem conclusion, thresholds, constants, compatibility relation, uniformity, and D-family dictionary remain open.

DML/Mathdoc exposes the Euclid item metadata and legacy `download/pdf_1/euclid.cmp/1103921545` endpoint; the current automation environment still receives HTML/anti-bot content rather than a clean PDF.
A 2026-06-28 direct attempt against Project Euclid's current PDF URL returned only a 1164-byte anti-bot HTML body with SHA-256 6368CFB9639DC2FB996F149B82D7D3F45C19882640E1C15E97571458562D81B3.
The next useful source commit should extract the actual Mayer/polymer convergence theorem and a translation lemma into Balaban D-families.

formulas.statement: Visual CMP116 Eq. (2.29) display already indexed elsewhere.
formulas.ascii: sum_D prod_{Y in D} alpha6 * exp(-delta*kappa*d_k(Y)) <= 1
Cammarota theorem conclusion + smallness thresholds/constants + DIndex/DParts dictionary + metric comparison
formulas.conclusion: What a source extraction commit must supply.

use_for: Orient agents to the Eq. (2.29) live source fields before opening any PDF.
Prevent treating the bibliographic Cammarota citation as closure.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite this operational card as a primary mathematical source.
Do not promote Cammarota CMP85 from bibliographic metadata to theorem input without clean theorem text.
Do not turn 'K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small' into a uniform Lean threshold without extracting dependencies.
open_questions: Exact theorem/lemma/proposition conclusion in Cammarota CMP85 beyond the extracted Eq. (1.4) premise.
Exact smallness condition, constants, compatibility relation, and uniformity.
Exact map from Cammarota polymers to Balaban D-families and Lean DIndex/DParts.

citations.summary: Extraction target for the Cammarota CMP85 general polymer Mayer-series theorem behind Balaban's Eq. (2.29)-type summability.
extracted_claims: Springer abstract says Cammarota proves convergence of the Mayer series for a general polymer model.
Balaban CMP116 cites Cammarota's argument for inequalities of the Eq. (2.29) type.
Archive-55 and Source-Dictionary cycle 42 visually confirm that Cammarota Theorem 1 on printed pages 517-518 has Eq. (1.4) as the first premise field, a two-body potential decay bound.

The visual check confirms the premise exponent is plain delta(x,y); the half-rate exp(-(1/2) delta(x,y)) belongs to the later Eq. (1.5) summability condition and Theorem 1 conclusion route, not this premise field.
The exact theorem, constants, compatibility relation, polymer weight and finite-volume uniformity must still be extracted from the primary text.

assumptions: delta is the source metric constrained in Eq. (1.5)
Archive-55 confirms Eq. (1.5) uses a half-rate summability condition, so consumers must not propagate the premise exponent as the theorem-conclusion rate
formulas.conclusion: This extracts one Cammarota source-premise field only; it does not prove the Mayer convergence theorem, Eq. (2.29), or any D-family dictionary.

formulas.statement: Schematic polymer-family convergence target to extract, not a source formula.
formulas.ascii: small polymer activities + compatibility graph -> convergent Mayer/polymer family sums
assumptions: general polymer model hypotheses from Cammarota CMP85
formulas.conclusion: A reusable summability theorem that Balaban adapts to Eq. (2.29).

assumptions: dictionary from Cammarota polymers to D-families

use_for: Give source agents a non-ambiguous extraction target for Cammarota.
Record that Springer abstract is metadata only.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite this operational card as a primary mathematical source.
Do not promote Cammarota CMP85 from bibliographic metadata to theorem input without clean theorem text.
Do not turn 'K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small' into a uniform Lean threshold without extracting dependencies.
Do not use ResearchGate OCR to set constants without visual checking against a primary page.
open_questions: Theorem 1 conclusion, full smallness hypotheses, constants, compatibility relation, polymer weight and finite-volume uniformity beyond the extracted Eq. (1.4) premise.
Smallness/fugacity condition and dependency on lattice dimension/metric.

citations.summary: Dictionary card from Balaban's source D-families around Eq. (2.26)-(2.29) to the Lean DIndex/DParts representation.
extracted_claims: Balaban fixes a connected component Y0 and sums over families D of localization domains Y.
Eq. (2.27) uses the union and connectedness of Y0 to compare summed distances.
Lean still needs the DIndex/DParts dictionary and the metric convention d_k.

formulas.ascii: D in SourceDFamily(Y0) <-> DParts(D) finite family, union DParts = Y0, admissible/disjoint/connected conditions
formulas.conclusion: Lean can apply Eq. (2.27)/(2.29) to R.DIndex.

formulas.statement: Metric comparison already visually located elsewhere.

use_for: Tell agents that Eq. (2.29) is not just a scalar theorem; it needs a D-family dictionary.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite this operational card as a primary mathematical source.
Do not promote Cammarota CMP85 from bibliographic metadata to theorem input without clean theorem text.
Do not turn 'K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small' into a uniform Lean threshold without extracting dependencies.
Do not identify repository DIndex with Balaban D-families by naming alone.
open_questions: Exact source predicate for D-families.
Whether the family is ordered, unordered, multiset, or finite set.
Exact disjointness/overlap convention of domains Y.

citations.summary: Threshold-dependency card for the qualitative CMP116 phrase 'K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small'.
extracted_claims: CMP116 states Eq. (2.29) for K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small.
Lean needs an explicit theorem package: existence of thresholds, dependencies, uniformity, or an explicit smallness hypothesis.
The threshold package must not depend on hidden finite-volume choices unless recorded.

formulas.ascii: exists K0 alpha6Max, K >= K0 and 0 < alpha6 <= alpha6Max -> Eq229Summability
formulas.conclusion: Formal replacement for qualitative sufficiently large/small phrase.

use_for: Force agents to record threshold dependencies rather than skipping them.

Do not turn 'K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small' into a uniform Lean threshold without extracting dependencies.

Which parameters may K0 and alpha6Max depend on?
Uniformity over scale, volume and background field.
